data.title: Short sellers reduce bets against S&P 500 technology stocks in July
data.description:
Short sellers reduced their bets against the information technology sector in July. The sector that holds the highest weightage on the S&P had an average short interest of 1.77% in July, down from 1.89% in June. 

The S&P 500’s information technology sector (NYSEARCA:XLK) has grown more than 15% so far this year, compared to an over 9% rise in the broader benchmark index. 

Enphase Energy (ENPH) was the most shorted stock in the sector in July, while Apple (AAPL) took the spot as the least shorted stock, same as last month.

Stocks with the largest and least short positions

Ranked by short interest as a percentage of shares float

Enphase Energy (ENPH), Super Micro Computer (SMCI), and Western Digital (WDC) were the top three most shorted stocks in July. Short interest in ENPH rose to 23.22% in July from 20.97% at the end of June. 

While Super Micro Computer (SMCI) and Western Digital (WDC) had short interests of 17.46% and 9.52%, respectively. 

Apple (AAPL) was the least shorted stock in the sector last month, with a short interest of 0.71%, preceded by Microsoft (MSFT) and NVIDIA (NVDA), with short interests of 0.72% and 0.82%, respectively. 

Industry Analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

IT Services was the most shorted industry within the information technology sector, with 2.56% short interest in July, down from 2.78% in June.  

Technology Hardware, Storage, and Peripherals was the second most shorted industry with 1.92% short interest as of July 31st, compared to 1.90% at the end of June.  

Communications Equipment was the least shorted industry in the sector this month, with a short interest of 1.37%.

data.title: Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac IPOs could raise mortgage rates - report
data.description:
Allianz SE's Pacific Investment Management Co. warned that the Trump administration's proposal to sell new shares of Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) and Freddie Mac (OTCQB:FMCC) could result in higher mortgage rates if the debt issued by the mortgage giants loses the government's backing.

Libby Cantrill, head of Pimco's public policy, wrote that unless the sale is structured to preserve the government's commitment to financially support the institutions, investor demand may wane for the mortgage-backed securities that they sell, as reported by Bloomberg on Friday. That would end up making mortgages more expensive for millions of people in an era where home affordability is already a challenge.

Citigroup strategists estimated recently that mortgage rates are likely to increased by 0.1-0.2 percentage points after privatization. It is unclear at this point whether the government would completely privatize Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) and Freddie Mac (OTCQB:FMCC) through the proposed IPOs that could raise about $30B later this year. 

However, President Donald Trump and some in his administration have espoused releasing the mortgage guarantors from government control, which they've been subject to since the 2008 financial crisis.

Fannie (OTCQB:FNMA) and Freddie (OTCQB:FMCC) don't originate mortgages. Rather, they buy mortgages from lenders, bundle them and repackage them into mortgage-backed securities, which they sell. The government's support of the securities means that investors demand a relatively small spread for the securities, which helps to keep mortgage rates contained.

Both government-sponsored enterprises ((GSEs)) already trade publicly on the over-the-counter markets. 

As expressed in a note she wrote in May, Cantrill worries that privatizing the GSEs could break the current process. "Don’t fix what’s not broken:  We believe that keeping Fannie and Freddie in conservatorship would ensure stability and liquidity, and is ultimately a better deal for the U.S. taxpayer," she wrote.

Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) stock rose 2.8% and Freddie Mac (OTCQB:FMCC) shares climbed 1.4% in midday Friday trading.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: European markets end flat; traders eye Trump-Putin meeting, records second weekly gain
data.description:
Update: The post reflects European markets closing indices.

London (UKX) -0.42% at 9,139. 

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.07% at 24,359.

France (CAC:IND) +0.67% at 7,923.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) ended flat on Friday. The market remained optimistic about the outcome of the Trump-Putin meeting scheduled for later in the day.

European stocks recorded a second consecutive weekly gain, supported by steady investor confidence amid improving economic data.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 3 basis points at 4.32%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point at 2.79%.

UK's 10-year yield was unchanged at 4.70%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

Seeking Alpha Editor Khushi Singh contributed to this article.

data.title: Wingstop's Smart Kitchen is a 'game changer' for the company -- Raymond James
data.description:
Raymond James views Wingstop’s (NASDAQ:WING) AI-powered Smart Kitchen technology—which boosts efficiency and speeds order fulfillment—as a “game changer.” Combined with early signs of improved traffic, the firm upgraded the stock to Strong Buy from Outperform.

“We see a very favorable risk/reward from current levels,” said Raymond James’ Brian Vaccaro, who believes the stock’s current valuation is even more attractive at current levels.

As customers lean toward shorter wait times, Wingstop’s (NASDAQ:WING) new technology shows “very encouraging” signs of traction: improved comparable sales that outperform peers by 550 basis points; third party delivery comps in the Dallas/Fort Worth market outperforming the U.S. average by mid-single-digits; and customer satisfaction scores that are 8 points higher than units without Smart Kitchen -- all of which can be attributed to ticket times that have been cut by more than half versus historical averages.

Although the stock has retreated 14% despite solid second quarter results, Vaccaro believes this is due to choppy third-party data in recent weeks which could create a “modest risk” to third quarter comparable sales.

“That said, we remain confident that comps can begin to improve through September and accelerate further through Q4 as comparisons ease,” Vaccaro said.

Wingstop (NASDAQ:WING) earns mixed reviews from Seeking Alpha authors and Wall Street analysts with the former recently moving to a Sell rating, the latter as a Buy. Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, however, views Wingstop (WING) as a Hold with a Quant score of 3.15 out of 5.

data.title: Texas AG launches probe into Xcel Energy, other utility companies connected to wildfires
data.description:
Xcel Energy (NASDAQ:XEL) -1.5% in Friday's trading after Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton announced an investigation into Xcel and two other utility companies connected to the 2024 Smokehouse Creek and Windy Deuce fires.

The AG's office issued civil investigative demand letters related to the two fires that burned more than 1M acres, killed two people, destroyed hundreds of homes, and killed 12,500 cattle.

"Power facilities operated by utility companies in the area caused and contributed to the outbreak," Paxton's office said. "Some of these companies may have acted negligently by failing to conduct necessary infrastructure maintenance and possibly prioritizing environmental, social, and governance or diversity, equity, and inclusion agendas over safety."

data.title: Market Voices: Goolsbee on Fed rates, Buffet-UnitedHealth, Corning-T1
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements and remarks that could impact markets, sectors or individual stocks.

  • Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee said he wants to see more inflation data before he decides whether to vote to lower interest rates when the Federal Open Market Committee meets in September.

“It’s been a little mixed,” Goolsbee told CNBC on Friday, referring to the most recent inflation data reports. “I feel like we still need another one, at least, to figure out if we’re if we’re still on the golden path.”

“It all depends on the data and what’s the economic outlook. If we keep getting inflation reports like [previous] ones ... I would be very comfortable that, hey, the dust is out of the air, it looks like we’re still where we were, which is a strong economy with inflation coming back down,” Goolsbee added.

  • Warren Buffet's Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.A) (NYSE:BRK.B) has taken a significant stake in troubled healthcare company UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH).

Berkshire Hathaway said in an SEC filing that it now holds approximately 5 million shares of UnitedHealth, valued at around $1.57 billion as of June 30.

  • Corning (NYSE:GLW) and T1 Energy (NYSE:TE) are teaming up to establish a fully domestic supply chain for the production of solar panel components, which has been dominated by China. 

Under the deal, Corning will supply hyper-pure polysilicon and solar wafers made in its Michigan plant to T1 for the production of solar modules at T1's Texas facility.  

“This landmark supply chain agreement with Corning will help invigorate America with scalable, reliable, low-cost energy,” said T1 Chairman and CEO Daniel Barcelo, in a statement. “This is American companies building in America and protecting American energy security."

“Corning is proud to help meet the growing need for solar products made in the United States,” said AB Ghosh, chairman and CEO of Corning's Hemlock Semiconductor unit, in the same statement. “We’re accelerating the ramp of our advanced manufacturing capabilities to support a resilient U.S. solar supply chain."

data.title: Par Technology rises after rating upgrade at BTIG
data.description:
Shares of Par Technology (NYSE:PAR) rose about 2% on Friday after BTIG upgraded the stock to Buy from Neutral, noting that the company's pipeline will comfortably drive over three years of more than 15% growth. 

The firm has set a $65 price target on the stock.

Analysts led by Andrew Harte believe that Par's $100M sales pipeline will enable the business to comfortably drive more than 15% growth for the next several years. On top of that growth, Par is competing for three "megadeals" with top-20 restaurant brands, which are not included in the $100M sales pipeline and could help drive growth to over 20%.

The analysts think Par is proving to be the premier enterprise restaurant tech solution, similar to what Toast (TOST) is for small and medium-sized businesses, or SMBs, and as such deserves a premium multiple.

Par's stock has traded about 25% lower over the last month, first from investors thinking it may be stretching to acquire Olo (OLO) (which management denied), and then more recently from disappointing second quarter 2025 results and a reduced growth outlook to mid-teens, according to the analysts.

"We see the stock's recent pullback as an attractive entry point and think growth is more likely to exceed expectations than fall short during the next year," said  Harte and his team.

data.title: HHS reinstates childhood vaccines task force
data.description:
An HHS task force responsible for oversight of childhood shots that was disbanded in 1998 is coming back.

The Task Force on Safer Childhood Vaccines is responsible for childhood vaccine safety oversight. The panel was created by Congress.

It will be headed by National Institutes of Health Director Jay Bhattacharya.

The panel will work with the Advisory Commission on Childhood Vaccines to develop recommendations on the development of childhood vaccines that have fewer and less serious side effects than those presently on the market as well as boost adverse reaction reporting. 

The task force's first report to Congress is due in two years, with subsequent reports every two years.

"I fear we are going to be right back to where we were in the 1980s, when the making of these vaccines became more and more onerous for the companies who make them," Paul Offit, director of the Vaccine Education Center at the Children's Hospital of Philadelphia, told Axios. "Vaccines are going to be less available, less affordable, and there is going to be more fear."

In May, Children's Health Defense, an anti-vaccine organization previously chaired by HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., sued him in that capacity to revive the task force.

data.title: ICU Medical wins bullish view at Piper Sandler on upcoming product cycle wave
data.description:
Shares of ICU Medical (NASDAQ:ICUI) spiked on Friday after Piper Sandler initiated its coverage with an Overweight rating and a $145 price target, citing an imminent new product cycle wave for the infusion pump maker.

Analyst Jason Bednar argued that California-based ICU Medical (NASDAQ:ICUI) is well-positioned to capitalize on a “large replacement opportunity” in the infusion pump market, which he described as having plenty of tailwinds, including strict regulatory barriers and lasting mid-single-digit growth.

Noting the company’s recently approved infusion pumps, Plum Solo and Plum Duo, the analyst wrote: “For these pumps, and considering the RFP/sales discussion timeline, management has referenced the upgrade cycles as commencing later this year and into next year.” “And we anticipate the launch of Solo and Duo could have a nice multi-year tail given the replacement needs across the market,” he added.

Bednar also noted that ICU Medical’s (NASDAQ:ICUI) recently submitted regulatory filings for the CADD ambulatory pump and Medfusion syringe pump give it “a host of new pump launches that can help carry revenue growth for the business over at least the next few years.”

Given potential improvements for ICUI’s gross margin and free cash flow, the analyst argues that the stock deserves an NTM P/E multiple of at least the upper teens compared to the five- and 10-year averages of 23x and 26x, respectively.
